rows.note: Structural massless gauge sector.
Structural massless color gauge sector. Free gluons are confined; there is no direct measured gluon rest mass entry comparable to other particle masses.

Current row uses the exact D10 running-family W output. The local calibration lane now treats this running family as the honest single-P electroweak core, and the current exactness defect is no longer mixed-family reporting alone: after the active selector fix, the next live mover is one coherent shared scalar package `Sigma_EW_D10 = (delta_alpha, delta_kappa, delta_rho, delta_rW)` with a zero-normalized readout family beneath `EWTransportKernel_D10`. The smaller inner gate is still scalar provenance equality: `delta_alpha`, `delta_kappa`, `delta_rho`, and `delta_rW` must expose one common family source, one scheme, and one origin kernel. (AMOROSO 2024)

Current row now follows the local active-readout selector rather than the old Stage-3 Z-only surrogate. Until one coherent pole/effective family is certified, `EWActiveReadoutSelector_D10` keeps the full electroweak quintet on the exact D10 running family. The public missing object for a promoted pole/effective family is still `EWTransportKernel_D10`, but the next live mover beneath that boundary is one shared scalar package `Sigma_EW_D10` and its zero-normalized readout family rather than another family split. The smaller exact clause beneath it is `EWTransportReadoutCoherence_D10`, and the immediate smaller gate inside that criterion is `EWScalarProvenanceEquality_D10`, requiring one common provenance triple for the four electroweak correction scalars.

D11 critical-surface branch. The local exactness sidecar now treats the synchronized D11 core as frozen and reduces the remaining mass-moving burden to one common readout object `CriticalSurfaceReadoutKernel_D11`, which should emit the shared low-scale vector `Theta_D11_HT(mu_t) = (delta_y_t, delta_lambda)` instead of separate post-hoc Higgs and top residuals. The current reduced live family is a rank-one relative-core ray with candidate `kappa_HT = 16/9`, and the tightest current live law is the two-estimator seed equality `sigma_y = sigma_lambda`, leaving the shared scalar seed `sigma_D11_HT` as the smallest remaining D11 mover. Blocked by: papers.compact.e.28-make-the-higgs-top-critical-surface-fully-rigorous.

Current charged-lepton continuation branch; the `code/particles/leptons` lane now carries the `C_e_hat -> g_e -> Y_e` split plus a shared charged-budget certificate surface whose constructive candidate is `sigma_seed = mean(family_eigenvalues) + min(spectral_gaps)` on the canonical decomposable `u+d+e` family. The smallest current live witness is now the explicit `charged_common_refinement_mean_eigenvalue_certificate`; the best reduced family inside it is the scalar-part subclause `common_refinement_preserves_mean_eigenvalue`, and once that closes against the proxy-supported min-gap side, the current sector equalizer and gluing norm should collapse and the stored shared absolute scale can promote. Blocked by: papers.compact.e.29-derive-the-yukawa-excitation-dictionary, papers.compact.e.30-replace-koide-assisted-lepton-fitting-with-a-theorem.

Current row remains the D6 capacity estimate for the public flavor-labeled table, not a closed neutrino sector. Separately, the `code/particles/neutrino` lane now exports a forward-usable `selector_law_standard_math_forward` bundle on the centered selector-law branch, with actual eigenmasses/splittings/order printed in `forward_neutrino_closure_bundle.json`. The strongest OPH-only promotion route still reduces to same-label overlap nonvanishing on realized refinement arrows beneath the selector-centered edge-bundle descent, and the next mass-moving object is now explicitly the defect-weighted `mu_e` family driven by `oph_same_label_overlap_defect_weight_normalizer`; its best reduced family is a positive raw edge score `q_e` followed by the canonical centered-log and mean-preserving lift to `mu_e`, which is the first local object that can lift the current exact 1-2 near-degeneracy from isotropic `mu_nu`. PMNS closure remains open and the sandbox is not yet the canonical predictor. Individual flavor neutrino masses are not directly measured as standalone masses; PDG quotes oscillation data, effective masses, and cosmological bounds instead. Comparison is qualitative until splittings and mixing close without PMNS import. Blocked by: papers.compact.e.32-derive-neutrino-masses-from-screen-capacity-as-a-theorem, particles.e.32b-close-neutrino-splittings-and-mixing.

Texture-continuation branch; the `code/particles/flavor` lane now carries an explicit projective polar-Riesz common-refinement eigenline transport readout whose upstream origin is reduced to a centered compressed branch-generator candidate on the realized three-generation charged bundle, with the off-diagonal edge cocycle treated as an induced downstream object rather than a primitive transport law. It has also replaced the old hand-tuned quark odd constants with a coefficient-free single-kappa projector ray on a shape-only odd codomain, and the local quark norm is already numerically pinned by the shared charged scalarization candidate. The current mass-moving missing object is now the canonical sector-even family excitation evaluator `oph_family_excitation_evaluator`, and the strongest reduced live family is exact: on a simple ordered three-point spectrum, every self-adjoint trace-zero diagonal sector-even evaluator is quadratic in `X_ord`. That evaluator should emit `E_u_log` and `E_d_log` and generate the large family hierarchy now missing from the near-degenerate sandbox singular values. Compare with scheme caveats. PDG quark references are running masses, not direct free-particle pole masses. Blocked by: papers.compact.e.29-derive-the-yukawa-excitation-dictionary, papers.compact.e.31-derive-quark-textures-ckm-mixing-and-cp-from-overlap-defects.

Main row uses the D11 pole branch; a separate D12 texture branch also emits m_t. The local exactness sidecar now treats the synchronized D11 core as frozen and reduces the remaining mass-moving burden to one common readout object `CriticalSurfaceReadoutKernel_D11`, which should emit the shared low-scale vector `Theta_D11_HT(mu_t) = (delta_y_t, delta_lambda)` instead of separate post-hoc Higgs and top residuals. The current reduced live family is a rank-one relative-core ray with candidate `kappa_HT = 16/9`, and the tightest current live law is the two-estimator seed equality `sigma_y = sigma_lambda`, leaving the shared scalar seed `sigma_D11_HT` as the smallest remaining D11 mover. D12 texture sidecar currently gives m_t = 174.490702 GeV. Blocked by: papers.compact.e.28-make-the-higgs-top-critical-surface-fully-rigorous.
